title: EGFR inhibitor resistance mechanisms in non-small cell lung cancer
abstract: EGFR inhibitor resistance involves secondary mutations, bypass signaling, and histologic transformation.

chunks.content: Acquired resistance to EGFR inhibitors often includes secondary EGFR mutations such as T790M, bypass pathway activation, and phenotypic transformation.
Third-generation inhibitors such as osimertinib were developed to address T790M-mediated resistance while maintaining activity in EGFR-driven disease.

title: Clinical and pharmacologic profile of gefitinib
abstract: Gefitinib is a first-generation EGFR inhibitor with established targeted therapy use.

chunks.content: Gefitinib is an orally available EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor used as a reference first-generation EGFR-targeted therapy.

title: PARP inhibition and synthetic lethality in DNA repair deficient tumors
abstract: PARP inhibitors exploit DNA repair weakness and support precision oncology workflows.

chunks.content: Olaparib is a clinically important PARP inhibitor and a major example of synthetic lethality translated into oncology practice.
